records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3568 - Imam (as) said: "He is not from us who abandons his worldly life for his Hereafter, nor his Hereafter for his worldly life."
records.arabicText: 3568 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لَيْسَ مِنَّا مَنْ تَرَكَ دُنْيَاهُ لآِخِرَتِهِ وَ لاَ آخِرَتَهُ لِدُنْيَاهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3568 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He is not from us who abandons his worldly life for his Hereafter, nor his Hereafter for his worldly life."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3569 - It is narrated from the Imam (as) that he said:
"Work for your worldly life as if you will live forever, and work for your Hereafter as if you will die tomorrow."
records.arabicText: 3569 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْعَالِمِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "اِعْمَلْ لِدُنْيَاكَ كَأَنَّكَ تَعِيشُ أَبَداً وَ اِعْمَلْ لآِخِرَتِكَ كَأَنَّكَ تَمُوتُ غَداً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3569 - It is narrated from the Imam (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Work for your worldly life as if you will live forever, and work for your Hereafter as if you will die tomorrow."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3570 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Wealth is an excellent aid in attaining piety towards Allah (swt)."
records.arabicText: 3570 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "نِعْمَ اَلْعَوْنُ عَلَى تَقْوَى اَللَّهِ اَلْغِنَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3570 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Wealth is an excellent aid in attaining piety towards Allah (swt)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3571 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, loves traveling in pursuit of sustenance."
records.arabicText: 3571 - وَ رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى لَيُحِبُّ اَلاِغْتِرَابَ فِي طَلَبِ اَلرِّزْقِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3571 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, loves traveling in pursuit of sustenance."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3572 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Set out (on a journey), and sustenance will set out for you."
records.arabicText: 3572 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اِشْخَصْ يَشْخَصْ لَكَ اَلرِّزْقُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3572 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Set out (on a journey), and sustenance will set out for you."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3573 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"I love to see a man striving in pursuit of sustenance. Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: 'O Allah (swt), bless my nation in their early mornings.'"
records.arabicText: 3573 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْعَزِيزِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّ أَنْ أَرَى اَلرَّجُلَ مُتَحَرِّفاً فِي طَلَبِ اَلرِّزْقِ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ -: " اَللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لِأُمَّتِي فِي بُكُورِهَا " ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3573 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I love to see a man striving in pursuit of sustenance. Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: 'O Allah (swt), bless my nation in their early mornings.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3574 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"If any of you has a need, let him seek it early in the morning, for I asked my Lord (azj), the Almighty and Glorious, to bless my nation in their early mornings."
records.arabicText: 3574 - وَ قَالَ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "إِذَا أَرَادَ أَحَدُكُمُ اَلْحَاجَةَ فَلْيُبَكِّرْ إِلَيْهَا فَإِنِّي سَأَلْتُ رَبِّي عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَنْ يُبَارِكَ لِأُمَّتِي فِي بُكُورِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3574 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If any of you has a need, let him seek it early in the morning, for I asked my Lord (azj), the Almighty and Glorious, to bless my nation in their early mornings."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3575 - Imam (as) said:
"If any of you intends to fulfill a need, let him seek it early in the morning and hasten in walking towards it."
records.arabicText: 3575 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِذَا أَرَادَ أَحَدُكُمُ اَلْحَاجَةَ فَلْيُبَكِّرْ إِلَيْهَا وَ لْيُسْرِعِ اَلْمَشْيَ إِلَيْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3575 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If any of you intends to fulfill a need, let him seek it early in the morning and hasten in walking towards it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3576 - Hammad al-Lahham narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Do not be lazy in seeking your livelihood, for our fathers used to strive for it and pursue it."
records.arabicText: 3576 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ اَللَّحَّامُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ تَكْسَلُوا فِي طَلَبِ مَعَايِشِكُمْ فَإِنَّ آبَاءَنَا كَانُوا يَرْكُضُونَ فِيهَا وَ يَطْلُبُونَهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3576 - Hammad al-Lahham narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not be lazy in seeking your livelihood, for our fathers used to strive for it and pursue it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3577 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, sent a man to fulfill a need, and the man was walking in the sun.
So the Prophet (sw) said to him: "Walk in the shade, for the shade is blessed."
records.arabicText: 3577 - وَ أَرْسَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ رَجُلاً فِي حَاجَةٍ فَكَانَ يَمْشِي فِي اَلشَّمْسِ فَقَالَ لَهُ "اِمْشِ فِي اَلظِّلِّ فَإِنَّ اَلظِّلَّ مُبَارَكٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3577 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, sent a man to fulfill a need, and the man was walking in the sun.
So the Prophet (sw) said to him:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Walk in the shade, for the shade is blessed."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3578 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever goes to fulfill a need without being in a state of ablution, and his need is not fulfilled, let him blame none but himself."
records.arabicText: 3578 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ ذَهَبَ فِي حَاجَةٍ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ فَلَمْ تُقْضَ حَاجَتُهُ فَلاَ يَلُومَنَّ إِلاَّ نَفْسَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3578 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever goes to fulfill a need without being in a state of ablution, and his need is not fulfilled, let him blame none but himself."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3579 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
"I find myself despising a man who faces difficulties in earning a livelihood, yet he lies on his back and says, 'O Allah (swt), provide for me,' while neglecting to go out into the land and seek Allah's (swt) bounty. Even the smallest ant comes out of its hole to seek its sustenance."
records.arabicText: 3579 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنِّي أَجِدُنِي أَمْقُتُ اَلرَّجُلَ يَتَعَذَّرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلْمَكَاسِبُ فَيَسْتَلْقِي عَلَى قَفَاهُ وَ يَقُولُ اَللَّهُمَّ اُرْزُقْنِي وَ يَدَعُ أَنْ يَنْتَشِرَ فِي اَلْأَرْضِ وَ يَلْتَمِسَ مِنْ فَضْلِ اَللَّهِ وَ اَلذَّرَّةُ تَخْرُجُ مِنْ جُحْرِهَا تَلْتَمِسُ رِزْقَهَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3579 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I find myself despising a man who faces difficulties in earning a livelihood, yet he lies on his back and says, 'O Allah (swt), provide for me,' while neglecting to go out into the land and seek Allah's (swt) bounty. Even the smallest ant comes out of its hole to seek its sustenance."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3580 - Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, loves the trustworthy worker."
records.arabicText: 3580 - وَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى يُحِبُّ اَلْمُحْتَرِفَ اَلْأَمِينَ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3581 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Udhafir, from his father, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as), handed me seven hundred dinars and said:
"O 'Udhafir, invest it in something."
Imam (as) then added: "I am not doing this out of greed, but I wish for Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, to see me seeking His bounties."
Udhafir said: I earned a profit of one hundred dinars from it. So, while performing Tawaf, I said to him: "May I be your ransom! Allah (swt), the Exalted, has grant a profit of one hundred dinars in it."
Imam (as) replied: "Add it to my capital."
records.arabicText: 3581 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُذَافِرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ : دَفَعَ إِلَيَّ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ سَبْعَمِائَةِ دِينَارٍ وَ قَالَ "يَا عُذَافِرُ اِصْرِفْهَا فِي شَيْءٍ مَا" وَ قَالَ "مَا أَفْعَلُ هَذَا عَلَى شَرَهٍ مِنِّي وَ لَكِنِّي أَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ يَرَانِيَ اَللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى مُتَعَرِّضاً لِفَوَائِدِهِ" قَالَ عُذَافِرٌ فَرَبِحْتُ فِيهَا مِائَةَ دِينَارٍ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ فِي اَلطَّوَافِ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَدْ رَزَقَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهَا مِائَةَ دِينَارٍ قَالَ "أَثْبِتْهَا فِي رَأْسِ مَالِي".

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3582 - Ibrahim ibn Abdul-Hamid narrated from Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
A man came to the Prophet, peace be upon him and his family, and said:
"O Messenger of Allah (swt), I have taught my son to read and write. In what profession should I place him?"
The Prophet, peace be upon him and his family, replied:"Entrust him to Allah (swt) and do not place him in five professions:
Do not make him a seller of shrouds, nor a jeweler, nor a butcher, nor a grain merchant, nor a slave trader."
The man asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), what is the harm in selling shrouds?"
The Prophet, peace be upon him and his family, explained: "The one who sells shrouds wishes for the death of my nation, while the birth of a new soul in my nation is more beloved to me than all that the sun has risen upon.
As for the jeweler, he engages in defrauding my nation.
As for the butcher, he slaughters so frequently that mercy departs from his heart.
As for the grain merchant, he hoards food against my nation, and it is more beloved to me that a servant meets Allah (swt) as a thief than that he meets Him having hoarded food for forty days.
As for the slave trader, Archangel Jibril (as) came to me and said: 'O Muhammad, the worst of your nation are those who sell human beings.'"
records.arabicText: 3582 - وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْحَمِيدِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ قَدْ عَلَّمْتُ اِبْنِي هَذَا اَلْكِتَابَ فَفِي أَيِّ شَيْءٍ أُسْلِمُهُ فَقَالَ "أَسْلِمْهُ لِلَّهِ أَبُوكَ وَ لاَ تُسْلِمْهُ فِي خَمْسٍ لاَ تُسْلِمْهُ سَيَّاءً وَ لاَ صَائِغاً وَ لاَ قَصَّاباً وَ لاَ حَنَّاطاً وَ لاَ نَخَّاساً" فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ وَ مَا اَلسَّيَّاءُ قَالَ "اَلَّذِي يَبِيعُ اَلْأَكْفَانَ وَ يَتَمَنَّى مَوْتَ أُمَّتِي وَ لَلْمَوْلُودُ مِنْ أُمَّتِي أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا طَلَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ اَلشَّمْسُ وَ أَمَّا اَلصَّائِغُ فَإِنَّهُ يُعَالِجُ غَبْنَ أُمَّتِي وَ أَمَّا اَلْقَصَّابُ فَإِنَّهُ يَذْبَحُ حَتَّى تَذْهَبَ اَلرَّحْمَةُ مِنْ قَلْبِهِ وَ أَمَّا اَلْحَنَّاطُ فَإِنَّهُ يَحْتَكِرُ اَلطَّعَامَ عَلَى أُمَّتِي وَ لَأَنْ يَلْقَى اَللَّهَ اَلْعَبْدُ سَارِقاً أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ يَلْقَاهُ قَدِ اِحْتَكَرَ طَعَاماً أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْماً وَ أَمَّا اَلنَّخَّاسُ فَإِنَّهُ أَتَانِي جَبْرَئِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ إِنَّ شَرَّ أُمَّتِكَ اَلَّذِينَ يَبِيعُونَ اَلنَّاسَ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3582 - Ibrahim ibn Abdul-Hamid narrated from Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man came to the Prophet, peace be upon him and his family, and said:
"O Messenger of Allah (swt), I have taught my son to read and write. In what profession should I place him?"
The Prophet, peace be upon him and his family, replied:"Entrust him to Allah (swt) and do not place him in five professions:
Do not make him a seller of shrouds, nor a jeweler, nor a butcher, nor a grain merchant, nor a slave trader."
The man asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), what is the harm in selling shrouds?"
The Prophet, peace be upon him and his family, explained: "The one who sells shrouds wishes for the death of my nation, while the birth of a new soul in my nation is more beloved to me than all that the sun has risen upon.
As for the jeweler, he engages in defrauding my nation.
As for the butcher, he slaughters so frequently that mercy departs from his heart.
As for the grain merchant, he hoards food against my nation, and it is more beloved to me that a servant meets Allah (swt) as a thief than that he meets Him having hoarded food for forty days.
As for the slave trader, Archangel Jibril (as) came to me and said: 'O Muhammad, the worst of your nation are those who sell human beings.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3583 - Sadir al-Sayrafi narrated:
I said to Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"A report has reached me about Hasan al-Basri, and if it is true, then 'Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we shall return.'"
Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) asked: "What is it?"
I said: "I have heard that Hasan used to say, 'If my head boiled from the heat of the sun, I would not seek shade under the wall of a moneychanger, and if my liver dried up from thirst, I would not drink water earned through trade. Yet, this (trade) is my occupation, my business, and by it my flesh and blood have grown, and from it, I performed my Hajj and Umrah.'"
Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) then sat up and said: "Hasan has lied! Take what is fair and give what is fair, and when the time for prayer arrives, leave what is in your hands and rise for prayer. Do you not know that the companions of the cave (Ashab al-Kahf) were moneychangers?"
records.arabicText: 3583 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ سَدِيرٍ اَلصَّيْرَفِيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ حَدِيثٌ بَلَغَنِي عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْبَصْرِيِّ فَإِنْ كَانَ حَقّاً فَ‍ "إِنّٰا لِلّٰهِ وَ إِنّٰا إِلَيْهِ رٰاجِعُونَ" قَالَ "وَ مَا هُوَ" قُلْتُ بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ اَلْحَسَنَ كَانَ يَقُولُ لَوْ غَلَى دِمَاغُهُ مِنْ حَرِّ اَلشَّمْسِ مَا اِسْتَظَلَّ بِحَائِطِ صَيْرَفِيٍّ وَ لَوْ تَفَرَّثَتْ كَبِدُهُ مَاءً وَ هُوَ عَمَلِي وَ تِجَارَتِي وَ عَلَيْهِ نَبَتَ لَحْمِي وَ دَمِي وَ مِنْهُ حَجَّتِي وَ عُمْرَتِي قَالَ فَجَلَسَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ثُمَّ قَالَ "كَذَبَ اَلْحَسَنُ خُذْ سَوَاءً وَ أَعْطِ سَوَاءً فَإِذَا حَضَرَتِ اَلصَّلاَةُ فَدَعْ مَا بِيَدِكَ وَ اِنْهَضْ إِلَى اَلصَّلاَةِ أَ مَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَابَ اَلْكَهْفِ كَانُوا صَيَارِفَةً ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3583 - Sadir al-Sayrafi narrated:
I said to Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A report has reached me about Hasan al-Basri, and if it is true, then 'Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we shall return.'"
Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) asked: "What is it?"
I said: "I have heard that Hasan used to say, 'If my head boiled from the heat of the sun, I would not seek shade under the wall of a moneychanger, and if my liver dried up from thirst, I would not drink water earned through trade. Yet, this (trade) is my occupation, my business, and by it my flesh and blood have grown, and from it, I performed my Hajj and Umrah.'"
Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) then sat up and said: "Hasan has lied! Take what is fair and give what is fair, and when the time for prayer arrives, leave what is in your hands and rise for prayer. Do you not know that the companions of the cave (Ashab al-Kahf) were moneychangers?"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3584 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Woe to the merchants of my nation because of (their frequent oaths) 'No, by Allah (swt)' and 'Yes, by Allah (swt).' And woe to the craftsmen of my nation because of (their worries about) today and tomorrow."
records.arabicText: 3584 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "وَيْلٌ لِتُجَّارِ أُمَّتِي مِنْ لاَ وَ اَللَّهِ وَ بَلَى وَ اَللَّهِ وَ وَيْلٌ لِصُنَّاعِ أُمَّتِي مِنَ اَلْيَوْمِ وَ غَدٍ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3584 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Woe to the merchants of my nation because of (their frequent oaths) 'No, by Allah (swt)' and 'Yes, by Allah (swt).' And woe to the craftsmen of my nation because of (their worries about) today and tomorrow."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3585 - Imam Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), narrated through Jabir from Amr ibn Shimr:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, underwent cupping. A servant from the tribe of Banu Bayadah performed the cupping, and the Prophet (sw) gave him payment. If it were prohibited, he would not have given him payment.
When the procedure was completed, the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, asked him: 'Where is the blood?'
The servant replied: 'I drank it, O Messenger of Allah (swt).'
The Prophet said: 'You should not have done that, but Allah (swt) has made it a barrier for you against the Fire.'"
records.arabicText: 3585 - وَ رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : قَالَ "اِحْتَجَمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ حَجَمَهُ مَوْلًى لِبَنِي بَيَاضَةَ وَ أَعْطَاهُ وَ لَوْ كَانَ حَرَاماً مَا أَعْطَاهُ فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ قَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "أَيْنَ اَلدَّمُ" قَالَ شَرِبْتُهُ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَقَالَ "مَا كَانَ يَنْبَغِي لَكَ أَنْ تَفْعَلَهُ وَ قَدْ جَعَلَهُ اَللَّهُ لَكَ حِجَاباً مِنَ اَلنَّارِ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3585 - Imam Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), narrated through Jabir from Amr ibn Shimr:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, underwent cupping. A servant from the tribe of Banu Bayadah performed the cupping, and the Prophet (sw) gave him payment. If it were prohibited, he would not have given him payment.
When the procedure was completed, the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, asked him: 'Where is the blood?'
The servant replied: 'I drank it, O Messenger of Allah (swt).'
The Prophet said: 'You should not have done that, but Allah (swt) has made it a barrier for you against the Fire.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3586 - It is narrated from Ali ibn Ja'far, from his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
"I asked him about scattered treats such as sugar, almonds, and similar items-whether it is permissible to eat them.
Imam (as) replied: 'It is disliked to consume anything that is seized unlawfully.'"
records.arabicText: 3586 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَخِيهِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلنُّثَارِ مِنَ اَلسُّكَّرِ وَ اَللَّوْزِ وَ أَشْبَاهِهِ أَ يَحِلُّ أَكْلُهُ فَقَالَ "يُكْرَهُ كُلُّ مَالٍ يُنْتَهَبُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3586 - It is narrated from Ali ibn Ja'far, from his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about scattered treats such as sugar, almonds, and similar items-whether it is permissible to eat them.
Imam (as) replied: 'It is disliked to consume anything that is seized unlawfully.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3587 - It is narrated from Amr ibn Shimr, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"When Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, revealed the verse: 'Indeed, intoxicants, gambling, [sacrificing on] stone alters [to other than Allah (swt)], and divining arrows are but defilement from the work of Satan, so avoid them' (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:90).
The people asked: 'O Messenger of Allah (swt), what is gambling (maysir)?'
He (sw) replied: 'Everything that involves betting, even dice and walnuts.'
They asked: 'What are the stone alters (ansab)?'
He (sw) said: 'They are the sacrifices offered to their idols.'
They asked: 'What are the divining arrows (azlam)?'
He (sw) said: 'They are their arrows used for seeking decisions or division.'"
records.arabicText:
3587 - وَ رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لَمَّا أَنْزَلَ اَللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى:
۝ إِنَّمَا اَلْخَمْرُ وَ اَلْمَيْسِرُ وَ اَلْأَنْصٰابُ وَ اَلْأَزْلاٰمُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ اَلشَّيْطٰانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ ۝
قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ مَا اَلْمَيْسِرُ قَالَ "كُلُّ مَا تُقُومِرَ بِهِ حَتَّى اَلْكِعَابُ وَ اَلْجَوْزُ، " قِيلَ فَمَا اَلْأَنْصَابُ قَالَ "مَا ذَبَحُوا لآِلِهَتِهِمْ " قِيلَ فَمَا اَلْأَزْلاَمُ قَالَ "قِدَاحُهُمُ اَلَّتِي يَسْتَقْسِمُونَ بِهَا " ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3587 - It is narrated from Amr ibn Shimr, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, revealed the verse: 'Indeed, intoxicants, gambling, [sacrificing on] stone alters [to other than Allah (swt)], and divining arrows are but defilement from the work of Satan, so avoid them' (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:90).
The people asked: 'O Messenger of Allah (swt), what is gambling (maysir)?'
He (sw) replied: 'Everything that involves betting, even dice and walnuts.'
They asked: 'What are the stone alters (ansab)?'
He (sw) said: 'They are the sacrifices offered to their idols.'
They asked: 'What are the divining arrows (azlam)?'
He (sw) said: 'They are their arrows used for seeking decisions or division.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3588 - It is narrated from Al-Sakuni, from Abu Abdillah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that:
"He (as) used to forbid eating the walnuts that children bring from gambling, and He (as) said it is unlawful (suht)."
records.arabicText: 3588 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَنْهَى عَنِ اَلْجَوْزِ اَلَّذِي يَجِيءُ بِهِ اَلصِّبْيَانُ مِنَ اَلْقِمَارِ أَنْ يُؤْكَلَ وَ قَالَ هُوَ سُحْتٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3588 - It is narrated from Al-Sakuni, from Abu Abdillah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He (as) used to forbid eating the walnuts that children bring from gambling, and He (as) said it is unlawful (suht)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3589 - It is narrated from Ayyub ibn Al-Hurr, from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"There is no harm in the payment for a wailing woman who mourns the deceased, and the payment for a singer who escorts brides; there is no harm in it, as long as she is not someone whom men enter upon."
records.arabicText: 3589 - وَ رَوَى أَيُّوبُ بْنُ اَلْحُرِّ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَجْرِ اَلنَّائِحَةِ اَلَّتِي تَنُوحُ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ وَ أَجْرِ اَلْمُغَنِّيَةِ اَلَّتِي تَزُفُّ اَلْعَرَائِسَ لَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ وَ لَيْسَتْ بِالَّتِي يَدْخُلُ عَلَيْهَا اَلرِّجَالُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3589 - It is narrated from Ayyub ibn Al-Hurr, from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in the payment for a wailing woman who mourns the deceased, and the payment for a singer who escorts brides; there is no harm in it, as long as she is not someone whom men enter upon."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3590 - It is narrated from Aban ibn Uthman, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Four things are not permissible in four situations: treachery, embezzlement, theft, and usury. They are not permissible in Hajj, Umrah, Jihad, or charity."
records.arabicText:
3590 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ :
"أَرْبَعٌ لاَ تَجُوزُ فِي أَرْبَعَةٍ اَلْخِيَانَةُ وَ اَلْغُلُولُ وَ اَلسَّرِقَةُ وَ اَلرِّبَا لاَ يَجُزْنَ فِي حَجٍّ وَ لاَ عُمْرَةٍ وَ لاَ جِهَادٍ وَ لاَ صَدَقَةٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3590 - It is narrated from Aban ibn Uthman, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Four things are not permissible in four situations: treachery, embezzlement, theft, and usury. They are not permissible in Hajj, Umrah, Jihad, or charity."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3591 - Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in the earnings of a hairdresser if she does not stipulate a specific payment and accepts what is given to her. However, she should not attach the hair of one woman to the hair of another woman. As for the hair of goats, there is no harm in attaching it to a woman's hair. Also, there is no harm in the earnings of a mourner if she speaks the truth."
records.arabicText: 3591 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ بَأْسَ بِكَسْبِ اَلْمَاشِطَةِ إِذَا لَمْ تُشَارِطْ وَ قَبِلَتْ مَا تُعْطَى وَ لاَ تَصِلُ شَعْرَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ بِشَعْرِ اِمْرَأَةٍ غَيْرِهَا فَأَمَّا شَعْرُ اَلْمَعْزِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يُوصَلَ بِشَعْرِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ وَ لاَ بَأْسَ بِكَسْبِ اَلنَّائِحَةِ إِذَا قَالَتْ صِدْقاً ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3591 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in the earnings of a hairdresser if she does not stipulate a specific payment and accepts what is given to her. However, she should not attach the hair of one woman to the hair of another woman. As for the hair of goats, there is no harm in attaching it to a woman's hair. Also, there is no harm in the earnings of a mourner if she speaks the truth."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3592 - It is narrated: "She makes it permissible by striking one of her hands against the other."
records.arabicText: 3592 - وَ رُوِيَ "أَنَّهَا تَسْتَحِلُّهُ بِضَرْبِ إِحْدَى يَدَيْهَا عَلَى اَلْأُخْرَى ".
